# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Python data processing application. Start from a full Ubuntu base image and install Python, pip, curl, wget, git, vim, and other useful development tools. This gives us flexibility for debugging and running various scripts in production.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:latest  # Base Image (Start from a full Ubuntu base image)
LABEL maintainer "Your Name <yourname@example.com>"   # Label to provide your contact details for users of the project or in case you want others to know about it's author(es). - optional and only if different than repository owner is set  .
SHELL [ "/bin/bash --login " ]   	# Use bash as our shell environment. Set up a login loop, this will prevent any commands entered into stdin from executing immediately upon running the docker command which can lead to more complications in case of errors and exceptions occurring during setup / upgrade process  .
RUN echo 'deb http://ftp.de.debian.org/debian jessie-backports main' >> /etc/apt/sources.list   	# Add Jessie Backport repository - This is to use the new back port packages only, for existing package names that are not in stretch but need a minimum level of support (which includes security updates).
RUN apt update  									  # Update system before installing any software or performing other actions required by it. It will download and cache all necessary files which makes this step faster then usual docker images building process . This command should be run after the LABEL instruction to avoid a race condition on uninitialized variables in Docker's multistage build feature
RUN apt-get install -y python3 vim curl wget git  	# Install Python, pip and other dev tools. – using 'apt get', which is faster than manually writing the package list . This line also needs to be run after LABEL instruction so that it doesn't override our author’s name set previously in docker image
RUN python3 -m vim --version   			# Check if we have installed correctly. We will use Python 2 instead of Python 3 for this task because the above line won't work as expected . You can modify these instructions according to your requirements (e.g., upgrading pip or others). For example, apt-get upgrade command is used in place below which would help us manage upgrades at runtime
RUN python3 -m vim --version   		# Installing Python 2 version of VIM now we are ready for our data processing script! Now install it as a last step. Execute the following commands and replace <yourscript> with your actual file name or path to run that specific command from Dockerfile .
RUN chmod +x /path_to/<YourScript> 	# To make sure Python scripts executable within container, this line changes script permissions so they can be executed by docker. Replace '/path_to' above and 'yourscript', as described in last RUN statement with your actual file name or path to run that specific command from Dockerfile
ENTRYPOINT [ "./<YourScript>" ]   # This will ensure the python3 script gets called when container starts. replace < Your Script > below . — example 'python_script' for './examples/bash-simple.sh'. The line above should be placed before RUN and CMD if it’s not already defined or commented out in Dockerfile
